title: kitchenkit
tagline: Modular kitchen ops tools — standalone or as MCPs inside CulinaryOS.
description: kitchenkit is a suite of focused, role-specific apps for managing kitchen operations — prep tracking, inventory, shift management, and more. Each tool works independently for operators who need just one piece, or connects as an MCP into CulinaryOS for unified hub-level orchestration.
story: Not every kitchen needs a full platform. Some just need one tight tool that works. kitchenkit was built to solve that — task-specific apps that respect the way different roles actually use software, designed from the start to snap into CulinaryOS when the operation grows.
forWho: Kitchen managers, prep cooks, and food service operators who need targeted ops tooling — whether running standalone or inside the CulinaryOS ecosystem.

detailedSpecs.architecture: Suite of independent micro-apps, each scoped to a specific kitchen role or task domain. Apps expose an MCP-compatible interface so CulinaryOS can connect them as orchestrated modules within the main hub.
detailedSpecs.dataFlow: Standalone: app manages its own data store and surfaces a focused UI per task. As MCP: CulinaryOS calls kitchenkit modules via the MCP layer — task data flows into the shared hub and surfaces in the operator dashboard.
keyPatterns: Role-scoped app isolation — each tool targets a specific user type
MCP interface layer for CulinaryOS integration
Standalone-first design — no hub dependency required
SQLite per-app data stores with optional sync to CulinaryOS core
detailedSpecs.deployTarget: Railway (standalone) or embedded via CulinaryOS MCP layer
